sanskrit:
यत्रोपरमते चित्तं निरुद्धं योगसेवया।
यत्र चैवात्मनाऽऽत्मानं पश्यन्नात्मनि तुष्यति॥
transliteration:
yatroparamate cittaṃ niruddhaṃ yoga-sevayā |
yatra caivātmanātmānaṃ paśyann ātmani tuṣyati ||
literal_meaning: When the mind is completely restrained from material mental activities by the practice of yoga, and when, by the pure mind, one sees the self by the self and is satisfied in the self.
contextual_meaning: The verse describes the psychological milestone of samādhi: a state where cognitive noise ceases, self-referential awareness becomes lucid, and intrinsic contentment is no longer contingent on external objects or achievements.
psychological_state: Flow-state merged with self-transcendence; the default-mode network quiets, producing a non-dual awareness where observer and observed coincide, accompanied by a spontaneous, non-grasping joy.
trigger_scenario: A programmer debugging code late at night suddenly notices the silent gap between thoughts, feels an effortless presence, and realizes the problem is already solved without strain.
anchor_text: Still your mind to meet your true self and rest in unshakable contentment.
